key_highlights: Iran-US tensions dominate markets, with oil prices surging and geopolitical uncertainty driving volatility across equities, bonds, and commodities.
Intel's blockbuster Q1 earnings and guidance fuel a tech rally, pushing the Nasdaq 100 toward record highs, while AI euphoria persists.
Gold and silver are falling as oil prices soar, reflecting a risk-off rotation into energy and safe-haven currencies.
Tesla Q1 revenue rose and profit grew 17%, but stock fell on Elon Musk's comments about HW3.0 limitations.
DOJ drops investigation into Federal Reserve and Jerome Powell, boosting Treasuries and opening path for potential leadership change.
Private credit markets face scrutiny as Stifel's Kruszewski and Bloomberg highlight challenges, while Chick-fil-A sells $650M of private debt.
SpaceX IPO filing shows Elon Musk can retain board control, and Norway wealth fund is assessing whether to invest.
Stablecoin volume has quadrupled in under 3 years, with JPMorgan calling the $300 billion market just warming up.
European shares fall amid Middle East worries, while South Korea's 'ant investors' flock to U.S. equities.
Brazil blocks Polymarket in crackdown on prediction sites, and Brazil sets higher ethanol blend to stem fuel impact from war.

sectors.trend: Strong rally driven by AI optimism and Intel's blockbuster earnings and guidance. Nasdaq 100 heading for record highs. Meta cutting 8,000 jobs as AI spending grows. OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, pushing toward AI super app.
sectors.implications: AI and semiconductor stocks remain core growth drivers. Investors should overweight AI-exposed names but watch for valuation extremes. Intel's turnaround story is a key catalyst.

sectors.trend: Oil prices surge due to Iran war and Hormuz blockade, creating the biggest ever energy security threat per IEA. Goldman says Persian Gulf oil supply 57% below pre-war levels. Gas prices soar globally. Baker Hughes outlook remains cautious despite Middle East disruption.
sectors.implications: Energy sector is a clear beneficiary of supply disruption. Oil and gas stocks, especially those with exposure to US production (Enerflex, National Energy Services), are attractive. Renewable energy and ethanol blends (Brazil) gain policy support.

sectors.trend: Lockheed Martin posts lower quarterly profit as delays weigh. Boeing analyst report mixed. SpaceX IPO filing shows board control for Musk, and Norway wealth fund considers investment. Canada wants to stop sending defense dollars to the US.
sectors.implications: Defense spending remains elevated due to geopolitical tensions, but execution risks (Lockheed) and trade friction (Canada) create headwinds. SpaceX IPO is a major upcoming event.

sectors.trend: Private credit faces scrutiny: Stifel's Kruszewski highlights challenges, Bloomberg's 'Real Problem with Private Credit' article. JPMorgan warns investors moving to safety. Capital One pays $425M settlement. CVC's Maticmind tries to calm bondholders. Chick-fil-A sells $650M private debt. Norway's DNB Bank profit slips.
sectors.implications: Private credit markets are under pressure from rising defaults and liquidity concerns. Investors should favor high-quality, liquid credit. Bank margins are compressing (DNB). JPMorgan's warning suggests caution on risk assets.

sectors.trend: FDA approves first-ever gene therapy to restore hearing. Vaxcyte's VAX-31 enters critical trial stage. Kodiak Sciences and Xenon Pharmaceuticals have pipeline catalysts. Thermo Fisher beats quarterly estimates. Estée Lauder earnings expected.
sectors.implications: Biotech innovation continues to drive value. Gene therapy approval is a landmark event. Investors should monitor trial readouts for Vaxcyte and Kodiak. Thermo Fisher's beat confirms lab products demand.

sectors.trend: Tesla Q1 revenue rises, profit grows 17%, but stock falls on HW3.0 comments. Nike cuts 1,400 roles. JetBlue allegedly used private customer data for pricing. Chick-fil-A issues private debt. Sam's Club launches 1-hour delivery.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending remains resilient but cost pressures are evident. Tesla's growth is intact but technology transition risks (HW3.0) create volatility. Retailers are investing in delivery speed.

sectors.trend: Masco Corp analyst report. Home improvement demand steady but interest rate sensitivity remains.
sectors.implications: Housing market is stable but high rates cap upside. Masco is a defensive play with steady cash flows.

sectors.trend: CB1 Capital Management says eased cannabis restrictions biggest news for industry since Nixon.
sectors.implications: Regulatory easing could unlock significant growth. Investors should watch for federal rescheduling or legalization.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: Iran-US tensions, Hormuz blockade, energy security crisis
AI euphoria: OpenAI GPT-5.5, Intel earnings, Meta AI spending, SpaceX AI pivot
Central bank policy: DOJ drops Fed probe, Powell decision, rate cut path (Hassett), BOJ two-way risk
Private credit stress: Stifel, Bloomberg, JPMorgan warnings, CVC bondholder concerns
Stablecoin growth: $300B market quadrupling in 3 years, JPMorgan bullish
SpaceX IPO: Board control, Norway wealth fund interest, AI expansion
Trade and tariffs: Brazil ethanol blend, aluminum tariffs (Century Aluminum), Canada defense spending
Consumer financial health: Retirement concerns, young adults relying on social media for advice, CD and savings rates up to 4.1%
M&A and corporate actions: Netflix buyback, Warner Bros./Paramount merger, Spirit Airlines bailout, Eminence Capital shuttering
Regulatory crackdowns: Brazil blocks Polymarket, Kalshi fines politicians, JetBlue lawsuit, DOJ drops Powell probe

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation: Iran-US conflict and Hormuz blockade
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase allocation to energy, defense, and commodities. Reduce exposure to sectors reliant on stable oil prices (airlines, consumer discretionary). Use options for tail-risk hedging.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Private credit market stress and liquidity concerns
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor investment-grade credit over private credit. Monitor CVC, Sonrava Health, and other stressed names. Reduce exposure to leveraged loans and direct lending funds.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Central bank policy uncertainty: Fed probe dropped, Powell decision, rate path
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify duration exposure. Treasuries gained on DOJ news, but rate cut timing is uncertain. Consider floating-rate notes or short-duration bonds.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Inflation resurgence due to energy prices and supply chain disruptions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Allocate to inflation-protected securities (TIPS), commodities, and real assets. Reduce exposure to long-duration bonds and growth stocks with high valuations.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech sector valuation bubble and AI hype
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on high-flying AI names. Rotate into value and cyclical sectors. Use stop-losses on momentum-driven positions.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Corporate earnings disappointments and margin compression
risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on companies with pricing power and strong balance sheets. Avoid names with high debt and weak margins (e.g., Clorox, Lockheed Martin).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and legal risks: Brazil Polymarket ban, JetBlue lawsuit, Kalshi fines
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor regulatory developments in crypto and prediction markets. Avoid overexposure to companies with pending litigation.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Trade and tariff disruptions: Canada defense spending, Brazil ethanol, aluminum tariffs
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify geographically. Favor domestic producers in affected sectors (Century Aluminum, US energy).

opportunities.recommendation: Overweight energy and oil services stocks
opportunities.rationale: Persistent supply disruption from Iran conflict and Hormuz blockade, with Goldman showing 57% below pre-war supply. Enerflex, National Energy Services, and Baker Hughes are well-positioned.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Intel on earnings beat and strong guidance
opportunities.rationale: Intel's blockbuster Q1 and outlook signal a turnaround in semiconductor manufacturing. AI demand and US chip policy support long-term growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in SpaceX IPO when available
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX is expanding into AI, has board control for Musk, and Norway wealth fund is assessing investment. The IPO could be a transformative event.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to gold and silver positions on dips
opportunities.rationale: Gold and silver are falling due to oil surge, but geopolitical uncertainty and Fed rate cut expectations support a long-term bullish case. Use pullbacks to accumulate.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Thermo Fisher on earnings beat
opportunities.rationale: Lab products and biopharma services growth continues. Thermo Fisher is a defensive growth play with strong fundamentals.
JPMorgan says $300B market is just warming up. Stablecoin volume quadrupled in 3 years. Exposure through Coinbase or Circle could benefit.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in gene therapy and biotech innovation
opportunities.rationale: FDA approval of first hearing gene therapy is a milestone. Vaxcyte and Xenon have promising pipelines. Biotech M&A could accelerate.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to private credit and leveraged loans
defensive_moves.rationale: Stifel, Bloomberg, and JPMorgan all highlight challenges in private credit. Liquidity risk is rising. Move to investment-grade bonds or Treasuries.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price spike with airline and consumer discretionary shorts
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil surge will hurt airlines, delivery services, and consumer spending. JetBlue and Spirit are particularly vulnerable.
CD rates up to 4.05% and high-yield savings at 4.1% offer attractive risk-free returns. Treasuries gained on DOJ news. Prepare for volatility.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and retail
defensive_moves.rationale: Nike layoffs, Clorox downgrade, and JetBlue lawsuit indicate consumer weakness. Rising gas prices will squeeze spending.
AI euphoria is lifting stocks, but valuations are stretched. Meta cutting jobs while spending on AI suggests cost pressures. Take profits on high flyers.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets will remain volatile due to Iran-US tensions, oil price spikes, and uncertainty over Fed rate cuts. Tech may continue to rally on AI optimism, but energy and defense will outperform. Private credit stress could trigger a risk-off event. Treasuries are a safe haven.
market_outlook.long_term: If Iran tensions de-escalate, oil prices could fall sharply, benefiting consumer and airline stocks. AI and semiconductor growth (Intel, SpaceX) will drive long-term returns. Stablecoin and crypto adoption will accelerate. Private credit will face consolidation. Central bank policy normalization will support bonds.
key_catalysts: Iran-US peace talks outcome (Witkoff, Kushner in Pakistan)
Fed rate decision and Powell's future (DOJ probe dropped)
Private credit defaults and CVC/Sonrava developments
Oil price trajectory and Hormuz blockade resolution
OpenAI GPT-5.5 adoption and AI regulation
Brazil ethanol blend policy and aluminum tariffs
watch_list: Iran news and Hormuz shipping data
Fed speeches and Powell's next move
South Korea equity flows to US
